### start datetime REMOVE?
    import datetime
    dt = datetime.datetime.now()


    ### establish connection to azure files and create client
    from azure.storage.fileshare import ShareDirectoryClient
    from notebookutils import mssparkutils

# Required variables
storage_account = "<yourstorageaccount>"
file_share = "datalab" # The file share name
secret_name = "datalab-connectionstring"
key_vault_linked_service = "ls_kv" # This should be the name used in your Synapse linked service

# 1. Fetch the SAS token from Azure Key Vault
sas_token = mssparkutils.credentials.getSecret(key_vault_linked_service, secret_name)

# 2. Set Spark config to allow access using SAS
spark.conf.set(
    f"fs.azure.sas.{file_share}.{storage_account}.blob.core.windows.net",
    sas_token
)

# 3. Build the WASBS path
file_path = f"wasbs://{file_share}@{storage_account}.blob.core.windows.net/myfolder/myfile.csv"

# 4. Read the file
df = spark.read.option("header", "true").csv(file_path)
df.show()

creds = mssparkutils.credentials.getSecret(akv_name, secret_name, kv_linked_service)
    parent_dir = ShareDirectoryClient.from_connection_string(conn_str=creds, share_name=share_name, directory_path=directory_path)
